---
title: '2014/03/29 Journal Sat'
date: '2014-03-29T12:59:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: '(My Journal) Fed By Faith'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '4869492222625557068'
blogger_filename: '/2014/03/20140329-journal-sat.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/(My Journal) Fed By Faith/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Journal'
---

<br />
<h4>
VOD:</h4>
COME:<br />
<br />
<blockquote class="tr_bq" style="background-color: white; font-family: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; margin-top: 15px; padding: 0px;">
<a href="http://e.kcm.org/HM?a=ENX7CqkTRT6y8SA9MKJrO7vnGHxKL8Tw-vcStGb5lw8W0bBhOG5mpqVsje_Hhe-jNFG2" target="_blank">Faith to Faith</a><br /><br /><span style="background-color: white; color: #666666; font-family: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; font-size: 16px;">But if we [really] are living and walking in the Light as He [Himself] is in the Light, we have [true, unbroken] fellowship with one another. - 1 John 1:7</span><span style="background-color: transparent;">&nbsp;</span></blockquote>
<blockquote class="tr_bq" style="background-color: white; font-family: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; margin-top: 15px; padding: 0px;">
<br /><b>One area of our lives we believers need to particularly guard is the area of our relationships.</b> We simply have to heed the Word of God where they're concerned. Let's not fuss with one another and criticize one another so much that we hinder the forces that God has given us to make us successful in this world.<span style="background-color: transparent;">&nbsp;</span></blockquote>
<blockquote class="tr_bq" style="background-color: white; font-family: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif; margin-top: 15px; padding: 0px;">
<b>Strife also causes trouble in the spirit realm.</b> It opens the door to the devil. It keeps our prayers from being answered. It even keeps our angels from moving on our behalf!</blockquote>
<br />
<blockquote class="tr_bq" style="-webkit-background-clip: padding; background-clip: padding-box; border: 0px; color: #333333; font-family: 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, 'Liberation Sans', FreeSans, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: 21.600000381469727px; margin-bottom: 20px; padding: 0px;">
<br /><a href="https://www.bible.com/users/darrellwolfe/reading-plans/28-joseph-prince?day=137&amp;user_id=darrellwolfe&amp;version=1" target="_blank">JP Devo 137</a><br />Whichever the case, His back would have been reduced to a mass of bloody, mutilated flesh. That day, Jesusâ€™ blood flowed freely from His body for your deliverance from every kind of disease and physical affliction. God allowed every one of those stripes to fall on His Sonâ€™s body so that your body need not be scourged with diseases.<br />My friend, if you are sick or suffering from some physical condition, <b>know that Jesus took the scourging as full payment for you to be free of that condition</b>. He bore those stripes so that, today, there is no sickness, no disease left for you to bear. By His stripes you have been healed!<br />Thought For The Day<br />Jesus bore the terrible scourging to purchase for you deliverance from every kind of disease.</blockquote>
<br />
<br />
<br />
SIDE NOTE:<br />
<br />
<blockquote class="tr_bq">
<span style="background-color: white; font-family: Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;">Peter wrote to husbands in 1 Peter 3:7 to live considerately with their wives. He said, "...bestowing honor on the woman as the weaker sex, since you are joint-heirs of the grace of life, in order that your prayers may not be hindered." Husbands and wives. Parents and children. Co-workers. Fellow church members. All of us need to wake up to the danger of strife and start walking in love.</span></blockquote>
Women : Weaker - How they process emotion... men are "Hard" with each other. Rough house. Beat each other up for fun. They go into silent-solitary mode when upset. Or big angry mode when attacked. Women process emotion verbally. They tent to treat others and them selves more gently. This is why (more often than not but not always) the Mom is the one kids run to when hurt and in need of comfort and the Dad is the one kids run to when they want "Fun" and "Horse Play". They are not "Weaker" people, they are more tender. Softer. It's one of the things men LIKE about women, is their softer more tender nature (Physically and Emotionally).<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
HERE:<br />
<br />
<br />
<ul>
<li>Depression is a "condition". It's a sickness of a spiritual and mental/emotional nature. And Jesus paid the price for it. I CAN, SHOULD, WILL be free of it!&nbsp;</li>
<li>Strife causes all kinds of sin and problems. I need to find a way to live at peace with people, all people. Including bossess (seen and unseen "the man"), customers, co-workers, but also wife and kids.&nbsp;</li>
</ul>
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
DO:<br />
<br />
People will not always do what you think they ought to do. Get over it. If you don't like the referrals leave. If you aren't leaving shut up. Just be "present" to help as many people as you can. Be Jesus for them today. Then let sales fall where they fall.<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<h4>
Thankful:</h4>
<br />
I'm thankful for Flavia, Jack, and Gateway Church, Olivier, Kids, Etc.<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<h4>
Feeling:</h4>
I'm feeling frustrated with Connor. He just walked in to ask the same questions he asks EVERY MORNING. "Daddy, I'm bored. There's nothing to do. What can I do?" "Read a book, read your bible" "No. I want to play a TV game or watch a movie..." Ahhh so it's not that he's bored it's that he wants something he KNOWS he can't have because he's been told every morning for months he cannot do those things in the morning on school or church days (that's 6 days a week). I'm tired of answering this same question.<br />
<br />
Today I did something different though. Instead of argue about it. I said " I know. You're bored. It's not fun to be bored..." and then I hugged him and suggested he read a book or bible which is what I was doing this morning. (Bible). That was less confrontational and it seemed to have a better outcome.<br />
<br />
<h4>
What's Up?</h4>
STILL waiting to hear from TD Ameritrade. Honestly now it's frustrating a bit. I knew they might take last week to make decisions. But I'd kind of hoped to hear something by now. I guess I know mentally that I wouldn't hear anything until mid/late this week or next. I guess I'm part of that microwave generation.<br />
<br />
<div>
<ul>
<li>Traded the Guitars</li>
<li>Sold the Ford. to Troy Thronberry</li>
<li>Got an interesting letter from Olivier, that's another post.</li>
</ul>
</div>
<br />
Out of time. More later I guess.<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;">
<a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/fender-squier-and-kona-acoustic-322c01961993.jpg" imageanchor="1" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/fender-squier-and-kona-acoustic-322c01961993.jpg" height="200" width="400" /></a></div>
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<h4>
</h4>
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
<br />
